question: How does the wavelength of UV light correlate with its ability to penetrate the skin?
response: The wavelength of UV light inversely correlates with its penetration depth into the skin. UVA radiation (320-400 nm), with longer wavelengths, penetrates deeper into the dermis, damaging collagen bundles and contributing to skin aging. UVB radiation (280-320 nm), with shorter wavelengths, is mostly absorbed by the epidermis and is more likely to cause DNA damage and skin cancer. Thus, longer wavelengths (UVA) penetrate deeper than shorter wavelengths (UVB).
